script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'phase3b-flakiness-policy',
  description: '3B/S3: N-run flakiness characterization, quarantine flaky NON-governing tests, flakiness-bar doc, add dep-hash+license+secret scanning to CI (report-only, not required)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Setup', detail: 'worktree + correct oracle harness + inventory current CI/scanning' },
    { title: 'Characterize', detail: 'run the suite N>=3, identify flaky non-governing tests' },
    { title: 'Policy', detail: 'quarantine list + flakiness-bar doc + CI scanning (report-only)' },
    { title: 'Codex-Gate', detail: 'codex checks the policy + that governing tests are never quarantined' },
    { title: 'Record', detail: 'commit + push + PR' },
  ],
}
const PY = '/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python'
const WT = '/home/polaris/wt/phase-3b'

phase('Setup')
const setup = await agent(
  `Set up Plan V4 3B / deliverable S3 (flakiness policy before required CI).
1. cd /workspace/POLARIS && git worktree remove ${WT} --force 2>/dev/null; git branch -D chore/review-readiness-3b 2>/dev/null; git worktree add ${WT} -b chore/review-readiness-3b chore/review-readiness-phase1.
2. Inventory the current CI: read ${WT}/.github/workflows/*.yml — is CI report-only (continue-on-error / || true)? Is there any dependency-hash check, license scan, or secret scan (pip-audit/bandit/gitleaks/detect-secrets)? What's the current test command?
3. Identify the GOVERNING tests that must NEVER be quarantined: the oracle acceptance (tests/oracle/*) and any RACE/faithfulness/strict_verify tests. List them so the policy protects them.
4. Baseline collection: '${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -1' (16738/11).
Return: ci_report_only (bool), existing_scanning (list), test_command, governing_tests (list), baseline.`,
  { label: 'setup', phase: 'Setup', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['ci_report_only','existing_scanning','test_command','governing_tests','baseline'], properties:{ ci_report_only:{type:'boolean'}, existing_scanning:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, test_command:{type:'string'}, governing_tests:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, baseline:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Characterize')
const chars = await agent(
  `Characterize test flakiness in ${WT}. Run a BOUNDED, fast subset of the suite MULTIPLE times (N>=3) to find flaky NON-governing tests — do NOT run the whole 16738-test suite 3x (too slow); instead pick the unit + fast integration tests (exclude slow/api/live markers, exclude tests/oracle) and run them 3 times, capturing which tests PASS sometimes and FAIL other times (flaky) vs consistently pass/fail.
Command pattern: for i in 1 2 3; do ${PY} -m pytest tests/unit tests/polaris_graph -q -x=false -p no:cacheprovider -m 'not slow and not api and not live' --tb=no 2>&1 | tail -3; done  (adjust to real markers). Collect the set of tests with non-deterministic outcomes across the 3 runs.
Also note the 11 known pre-existing COLLECTION errors (import/env issues) — these are separate from flaky tests.
Return: the flaky test node-ids found (list, may be empty), consistently-failing non-governing tests, and the run-to-run pass counts. Do NOT quarantine anything governing.`,
  { label: 'characterize', phase: 'Characterize', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['flaky_tests','consistent_failures','run_pass_counts','notes'], properties:{ flaky_tests:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, consistent_failures:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, run_pass_counts:{type:'string'}, notes:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Policy')
const policy = await agent(
  `Write the 3B/S3 flakiness policy + CI scanning in ${WT}. Inputs: setup=${JSON.stringify(setup)}, flakiness=${JSON.stringify(chars)}.
1. docs/review_readiness/flakiness_policy.md: define the non-flakiness BAR (e.g. a test must pass K consecutive CI runs to be "stable"; the suite flips report-only→required ONLY after N days green). State the ABSOLUTE rule: the GOVERNING measurement (oracle RACE/faithfulness — ${JSON.stringify(setup.governing_tests)}) is NEVER quarantined; if it is unstable that is a BLOCKING stop. List any flaky NON-governing tests found (${JSON.stringify(chars.flaky_tests)}) as the tracked quarantine list (mark with @pytest.mark.flaky or a documented skip-list — do NOT actually delete them).
2. If flaky tests were found, add a tracked quarantine mechanism (a docs/flaky_quarantine.txt list + optionally a conftest marker) — non-governing only.
3. CI scanning (report-only, do NOT make CI required): add to the CI workflow (or a new report-only job) dependency-hash verification (pip check / pip-audit), a license scan, and a secret scan (detect-secrets or gitleaks) — all as continue-on-error/report-only steps. If the scanning tools aren't installed, add the steps with the install command + note they run in CI where the tools are available; do NOT fabricate scan results.
Keep it honest: no runtime code changes; CI stays report-only.
Return: files_written, quarantine_count, scanning_added (list), ci_still_report_only.`,
  { label: 'policy', phase: 'Policy', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['files_written','quarantine_count','scanning_added','ci_still_report_only'], properties:{ files_written:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, quarantine_count:{type:'integer'}, scanning_added:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, ci_still_report_only:{type:'boolean'} } } })

phase('Codex-Gate')
const gate = await agent(
  `Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate 3B/S3. Write /tmp/threeb_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/threeb_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -25' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
Plan V4 3B: flakiness policy BEFORE CI becomes required — N-run stability, quarantine flaky NON-governing tests (governing RACE/faithfulness NEVER quarantined), define the bar, add dep-hash+license+secret scanning, keep CI report-only until provably stable. Evidence: setup=${JSON.stringify(setup)}, flakiness=${JSON.stringify(chars)}, policy=${JSON.stringify(policy)}.
Ask codex: "Does this establish a sound flakiness policy that (a) protects the governing measurement from ever being quarantined, (b) tracks flaky non-governing tests without deleting them, (c) adds dep-hash/license/secret scanning as report-only, and (d) keeps CI report-only (not flipped to required)? Any fabricated scan results or a governing test wrongly quarantined? End with 3B-OK or 3B-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.`,
  { label: 'codex-gate', phase: 'Codex-Gate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['verdict','codex_points'], properties:{ verdict:{type:'string'}, codex_points:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}} } } })

phase('Record')
const rec = await agent(
  `Record 3B in ${WT} (branch chore/review-readiness-3b). Commit only if codex=3B-OK (${gate.verdict}) and ci_still_report_only=${policy.ci_still_report_only}. Stage explicit paths (docs/ + .github/ + any quarantine list + conftest; NOT tests/oracle — guard git diff --cached --name-only | grep -c tests/oracle == 0). No-secret grep. Commit ("Phase 3B/S3: flakiness policy + report-only CI scanning (governing measurement never quarantined)") with standard trailers. Push -u origin; PR base gate-inversion.
Return {commit_sha, pushed, pr_url, committed_or_blocked}.`,
  { label: 'record', phase: 'Record', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['commit_sha','pushed','pr_url','committed_or_blocked'], properties:{ commit_sha:{type:'string'}, pushed:{type:'boolean'}, pr_url:{type:'string'}, committed_or_blocked:{type:'string'} } } })

return { setup, chars, policy, gate, record: rec }

existing_scanning: NONE of the required scan classes are present: no dependency-hash check (no pip --require-hashes / hashed requirements), no license scan (no pip-licenses/licensecheck/cyclonedx/SBOM), no secret scan (no gitleaks/detect-secrets), no SAST/vuln scan (no bandit/pip-audit/safety/semgrep/trivy).

Closest existing supply-chain hygiene (NOT the above): GitHub Actions are SHA-pinned in every workflow; web_ci.yml 'verify_pip_resolution' job runs `pip install --dry-run -r requirements.txt` and `-r requirements-v6.txt` to catch ResolutionImpossible early.

sha256sum usage in codex-required.yml / codex-visual-required.yml / codex_verdict_check.yml is Codex review-artifact diff-binding (hashing the PR diff/rubric/UI-surface tree), NOT dependency-hash pinning.
setup.test_command: pytest tests/v6/ --ignore=tests/v6/acceptance -q --strict-markers --no-header   (web_ci.yml pytest_v6_backend job, run with PYTHONPATH=src). This is the ONLY pytest that actually executes in CI. python_ci.yml (report-only) runs no pytest — only ruff/mypy/interrogate, all with `|| true` under a job-level `continue-on-error: true`. Local baseline collection uses: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q
governing_tests: tests/v6/acceptance/test_dramatiq_acceptance.py (RACE/broker acceptance — currently excluded from CI via --ignore=tests/v6/acceptance; needs a live broker)
setup.baseline: 16738 tests collected, 11 collection errors (matches expected 16738/11). Collection was Interrupted by the 11 errors; e.g. ERROR tests/unit/test_perspective_tracking.py. Command: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q (from /home/polaris/wt/phase-3b, branch chore/review-readiness-3b off chore/review-readiness-phase1). Worktree created at /home/polaris/wt/phase-3b, HEAD c5448ec 'Phase 1C docs: record migration status + 20 conflicting-default findings'.

chars.run_pass_counts: INCONCLUSIVE — the 3x serial sweep did not finish within the available time. Run 1 was still executing at ~70% when output was forced; runs 2 and 3 had not started. No run produced a completed pass/fail summary or JUnit XML (pytest emits the -rf FAILED node-id list and --junit-xml only at session end), so no per-run pass counts are available yet. Earlier apparent completions ("9256 passed", "RUN1 END rc=1") were confirmed PHANTOM background-task notifications — verified against ground truth (pgrep + serial.log + xml presence), the referenced processes were still running and no xml existed. A single serial run over this subset takes ~11-13+ min wall (the tests/polaris_graph ML tail — retrieval ~221, synthesis ~270, clinical_retrieval ~122, llm ~104 test funcs doing real CPU embedding/NLI inference — is a slow band that plateaued at ~68-70% for several minutes while confirmed CPU-active, not hung: ~7-8 cores busy, 326 threads, state R).
chars.notes:
SCOPE/METHOD: Ran `/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit tests/polaris_graph -p no:cacheprovider -p no:randomly -m 'not slow and not api and not live' --continue-on-collection-errors --junit-xml=runN.xml -rf -q` STRICTLY SERIALLY via a driver at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/serial_runs.sh (with a guard refusing to start if another pytest is running). tests/oracle was excluded per instructions (not in the selected paths). Markers present in pytest.ini: slow, api, live (no separate 'integration' marker). Subset selects ~9455 tests (5 deselected by markers).

CRITICAL PROCESS FINDING — CONCURRENCY CONTAMINATION: My first two runs accidentally overlapped (run 1 was still alive when I launched run 2). Two pytest sessions sharing filesystem/temp/port/model state produced a burst of ~90 early failures (FFFF clustered at 0-2%, in early-alphabetical polaris_graph dirs). These are ARTIFACTS of parallel execution, NOT real flakiness. I killed the contaminated run 2 and restarted everything strictly serial. Any future characterization MUST serialize — the suite is not safe to run concurrently with itself.

CRITICAL ENV FINDING — UNRELIABLE BACKGROUND NOTIFICATIONS: This environment's background-task/monitor completion notifications repeatedly fired FALSE/future events (e.g. 'RUN1 END rc=1 16:24:19' delivered while the wall clock was 16:14 and the process was still running; a phantom '9256 passed' summary). Ground truth must be taken ONLY from pgrep -f 'runN.xml', the driver's serial.log markers, and presence of runN.xml. Do not trust the notification text.

COLLECTION ERRORS — 35 (NOT 11): The task mentioned 11 known pre-existing collection errors; this worktree/branch (chore/review-readiness-3b) actually shows 35 collection ImportErrors in this subset (verified via --co). These are separate from flaky tests. By directory: audit_bundle (10), api (8), benchmark (8), unit (6: test_clarification_agent, test_hle_benchmark, test_mesh_api, test_mesh_cli, test_mesh_snapshot, test_perspective_tracking), plus audit_ir/test_manifest_augment, anti_sycophancy/test_stance_delta, and top-level test_demo_smoke. Full list captured. The count discrepancy (35 vs 11) is itself worth flagging — the recent config migration on this branch (commits: 'migrate 832 os.getenv call sites to central resolve()', 'central defaults registry') is a plausible source of new import/env breakage and should be investigated.

DELIVERABLE STATUS: flaky_tests=[] and consistent_failures=[] are returned EMPTY because I have NO completed multi-run data to substantiate either — reporting node-ids now would be fabrication. To complete this correctly, let the serial driver (still running; serial.log + trace.log + run1/2/3.xml under the scratchpad flaky/ dir) finish all 3 runs, then diff the three run*.xml files: node-ids failing in some-but-not-all runs = flaky; failing in all 3 = consistent non-governing failures. NOTE: the early-suite failures seen under contamination need re-confirmation under clean serial conditions before being classified — under load they were parallel-execution artifacts, but some may reproduce serially (potential real regressions from the config migration), which the completed run1.xml will reveal.

ARTIFACTS: driver=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/serial_runs.sh; live logs+xml=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/ (serial.log, trace.log, run1.log/run1.xml, etc.). No governing tests were quarantined or modified (characterization only).

codex_points: (d) PASS — CI remains report-only (ci_still_report_only:true; python_ci.yml runs only ruff/mypy/interrogate with `|| true` under continue-on-error; the sole executing CI pytest is the pre-existing web_ci.yml v6 backend job). CI was not flipped to required.

(c) PARTIAL — license (pip-licenses) and secret (detect-secrets) scanning added report-only, plus pip-audit (vuln/CVE) and pip check (integrity). But the specifically-requested DEPENDENCY-HASH class is STILL MISSING: pip check only validates installed-package compatibility and pip-audit only checks vulnerabilities; neither verifies cryptographic dependency hashes (no pip --require-hashes / hashed requirements). This blocks the gate.

(b) HONEST but UNPROVEN — empty flaky_tests=[] / consistent_failures=[] with quarantine_count:0 is the correct, non-fabricated outcome given no completed multi-run data (reporting node-ids would be fabrication). However the 3x serial characterization never finished (run 1 ~70%, runs 2-3 never started; earlier 'completions' were phantom background notifications), so flakiness and the stability bar remain unproven. This blocks the gate.

(a) PASS-on-conduct but NOT-PROVEN-by-artifact — no governing test was quarantined (characterization only, nothing modified), but permanent protection of governing tests is not proven because the flakiness_policy.md contents were not shown to the reviewer.
NO FABRICATION and NO WRONG QUARANTINE — codex confirms no fabricated scan results are presented (no scan results presented at all), and no governing test was wrongly quarantined.

SEPARATE FLAG (not gate-blocking but noted): the subset showed 35 collection ImportErrors vs the 11 expected on full baseline; recent config migration ('migrate 832 os.getenv call sites to central resolve()') is a plausible source and warrants investigation.

TWO BLOCKERS to clear before re-gate: (1) finish the N-run characterization to produce real flaky/consistent lists and define/validate the stability bar; (2) add actual dependency-hash enforcement (hashed requirements / pip --require-hashes) rather than relying on pip check + pip-audit.

summary: 3B/S3: N-run flakiness characterization, quarantine flaky NON-governing tests, flakiness-bar doc, add dep-hash+license+secret scanning to CI (report-only, not required)

phases.title: Setup
phases.detail: worktree + correct oracle harness + inventory current CI/scanning

phases.title: Characterize
phases.detail: run the suite N>=3, identify flaky non-governing tests

phases.title: Policy
phases.detail: quarantine list + flakiness-bar doc + CI scanning (report-only)

phases.title: Codex-Gate
phases.detail: codex checks the policy + that governing tests are never quarantined
